metadata.purpose: RAG knowledge base for TARA pipeline — Driver Monitoring ECU domain knowledge
metadata.notes: Enriched with UNECE R157 ALKS requirements, AUTOSAR AP functional clusters, ENISA AI threat landscape

automation_level.description: DMS is mandatory at L3 per UNECE R157 ALKS — monitors driver availability and readiness to take over control

ecu_overview.primary_function: Monitors driver attentiveness, drowsiness, distraction, and hands-on-wheel status in real time. Issues transition demands when ADAS requests driver takeover and triggers Minimum Risk Maneuver (MRM) if driver is unresponsive.
ecu_overview.core_pipeline: Sensors → Driver State Detection → State Classification → Alert/Transition Demand → MRM if needed
ecu_overview.operating_environment: Real-time, safety-critical, continuous monitoring, integrated with ADAS and ALKS system
key_characteristics: AI-based driver state detection using IR/NIR camera
Real-time gaze, head pose, blink rate tracking
Transition demand handling per UNECE R157
Connected to ADAS ECU and VCU

r157_requirements.escalation_flow: System detects risk → Alert → Wait → Escalate → MRM

hardware.mcu: Mid-range SoC with AI accelerator for real-time face/gaze tracking

processing_units.role: AI inference for driver state detection — gaze, head pose, blink

sensors.function: Face detection, gaze tracking, eye blink monitoring, head pose estimation
Hands-on-wheel detection via steering torque measurement
Precise eye gaze direction and blink rate tracking

actuator_interfaces: CAN to instrument cluster for optical warnings
CAN/LIN to speaker for acoustic warnings
CAN to steering for haptic feedback
Ethernet to ADAS ECU for driver state output
Ethernet to VCU for MRM trigger
communication_interfaces: Ethernet (primary — ADAS ECU communication, ara::com SOME/IP)

memory.flash: NVM for driver state model and firmware
memory.ram: High-speed RAM for real-time AI inference
memory.persistent_storage: ara::per for driver profiles and calibration

software.os: POSIX-compliant OS (e.g., QNX, Linux with RT patch)

ara_functional_clusters.role: Communication between DMS and ADAS ECU, VCU, instrument cluster
features: E2E protection for safety-critical state messages
dms_use: Send driver state to ADAS ECU
Start driver monitoring when vehicle starts

ara_functional_clusters.role: Store driver profiles and calibration data
dms_use: Store driver-specific calibration for gaze tracking
Detect abnormal driver state data patterns
Monitor for camera feed spoofing attempts

ara_functional_clusters.role: OTA update handling for DMS firmware and AI models
dms_use: Driver state AI model OTA updates

ara_functional_clusters.role: Controls access to DMS services and camera data
dms_use: Restrict access to driver video stream

application_layer.function: AI-based detection of driver attentiveness, drowsiness, distraction using IR camera
Detects whether driver hands are on steering wheel via torque sensor
Classifies driver state into: Attentive, Distracted, Drowsy, Unresponsive
Manages transition demand from ADAS — issues warnings and escalates per R157
Triggers Minimum Risk Maneuver if driver is unresponsive after transition demand

attack_surface_per_stage.model_training: Data poisoning — wrong driver state labels
attack_surface_per_stage.model_inference: Camera spoofing — fake attentive driver feed

assets.description: Raw IR/NIR camera frames of driver face and upper body
assets.cybersecurity_property: Highly sensitive privacy asset — contains biometric data. Spoofed video can fool DMS into thinking driver is attentive when not.

assets.description: Trained AI model for driver state classification — attentive, distracted, drowsy, unresponsive
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered model can misclassify drowsy driver as attentive — directly causes safety failure

assets.description: Classified driver state sent to ADAS ECU
assets.cybersecurity_property: Corrupted state output disables transition demand — driver takeover not requested

assets.description: Transition demand request from ADAS to DMS and warning commands to driver
assets.cybersecurity_property: Suppressed or injected transition demand can prevent or falsely trigger driver takeover

assets.description: MRM trigger command sent to VCU to execute minimum risk maneuver
assets.cybersecurity_property: Injected MRM trigger causes sudden unintended vehicle stop. Suppressed MRM trigger prevents safety response.

assets.description: Driver-specific calibration data for gaze tracking stored via ara::per
assets.cybersecurity_property: Privacy asset — contains driver biometric calibration. Tampered profile degrades detection accuracy.

assets.description: DMS ECU software including OS, AUTOSAR AP middleware, and AI inference engine
assets.cybersecurity_property: Unauthorized firmware modification disables driver monitoring entirely

assets.description: Warning escalation logic — optical, acoustic, haptic sequence per R157
assets.cybersecurity_property: Tampered alert logic suppresses warnings — driver never notified of transition demand

data_flows.r155_reference: T5.1 — Code injection via communication channels
T5.1, T25.1 — Unauthorized access to vehicle key functions
T12.1 — Compromise of OTA update procedures

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker replays or spoofs a video of attentive driver to fool DMS AI model into thinking driver is alert
attack_examples: Replay recorded attentive driver video to camera feed
Place printed face photo in front of camera

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker injects or suppresses CAN messages to fake driver state or suppress warnings
attack_examples: Inject fake torque sensor reading — false hands-on-wheel
Suppress warning CAN message — driver not alerted

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker intercepts or manipulates driver state messages between DMS and ADAS ECU
attack_examples: MITM replaces drowsy state with attentive state
Inject false MRM trigger causing sudden vehicle stop

attack_surfaces.description: Malicious OTA replaces driver state AI model with backdoored version that always outputs attentive
attack_examples: Backdoored model always classifies driver as attentive

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker uses UDS over DoIP to disable DMS monitoring or extract driver video data
attack_examples: Write diagnostic command to disable DMS
Read driver video buffer via memory dump

attack_surfaces.description: Attacker accesses stored driver profile data containing biometric calibration
attack_examples: Extract driver biometric data via ara::per storage
Tamper calibration to degrade gaze tracking
attack_surfaces.r155_reference: T19.2 — Unauthorized access to privacy information

threat_catalog.title: Camera Feed Spoofing — Fake Attentive Driver
threat_catalog.description: Attacker replays a recorded video of attentive driver or places a printed face photo in front of IR camera to fool DMS AI model into classifying drowsy/absent driver as attentive

threat_catalog.title: CAN Message Spoofing — False Hands-on-Wheel
threat_catalog.description: Attacker injects fake steering torque CAN messages to make DMS believe driver hands are on wheel when they are not

threat_catalog.title: Driver State Message Tampering — MITM
threat_catalog.description: Attacker performs MITM on Ethernet between DMS and ADAS ECU, replacing drowsy driver state with attentive state, preventing transition demand

threat_catalog.title: AI Model Backdoor — Always Attentive Output
threat_catalog.description: Attacker delivers malicious OTA update replacing driver state model with backdoored version that always outputs attentive regardless of actual driver state

threat_catalog.title: Warning Suppression — Alert Logic Tampering
threat_catalog.description: Attacker tampers with alert logic via diagnostic interface or OTA to suppress optical, acoustic, or haptic warnings — driver never receives transition demand

threat_catalog.title: False MRM Injection
threat_catalog.description: Attacker injects false MRM trigger command to VCU causing sudden unintended vehicle stop in traffic

threat_catalog.title: Camera Blinding — IR Sensor Saturation
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses IR light source to saturate the DMS IR camera, blinding driver monitoring and disabling state detection

threat_catalog.title: DMS ECU DoS — Monitoring Disabled
threat_catalog.description: Attacker floods DMS ECU or its Ethernet interface causing processing overload and disabling real-time driver monitoring

threat_catalog.title: Driver Video Replay Attack
threat_catalog.description: Attacker records previous session video of attentive driver and replays it into camera feed to bypass monitoring

threat_catalog.title: Driver Video Stream Privacy Breach
threat_catalog.description: Attacker intercepts or extracts driver IR video stream containing biometric face data — major privacy violation

threat_catalog.title: Driver Profile Data Extraction
threat_catalog.description: Attacker extracts stored driver biometric calibration data from ara::per storage via diagnostic or physical access
threat_catalog.r155_reference: T19.2 — Unauthorized access to privacy information

threat_catalog.title: Diagnostic Session Exploit — DMS Disable
threat_catalog.description: Attacker uses UDS over DoIP to unlock extended diagnostic session and disable driver monitoring function or extract driver video data

security_controls.protects: Ethernet communication between DMS and ADAS ECU
CAN messages — torque sensor, warning commands
Driver state output integrity on ara::com events
DMS firmware integrity and cryptographic keys
Driver state AI model and firmware OTA updates
Runtime monitoring for camera spoofing and message injection
Driver video stream and diagnostic interface access
DMS AI model from video replay and photo spoofing attacks
Driver biometric video data from unauthorized access

iso21434_tara_context.item_definition: Driver Monitoring System ECU responsible for real-time monitoring of driver attentiveness and managing transition demands at SAE L2-L3 per UNECE R157
iso21434_tara_context.item_boundary: Includes IR camera, steering torque sensor, seat sensor, AI driver state classifier, transition demand manager, MRM trigger, and interfaces to ADAS ECU and VCU
cybersecurity_goals: Driver video stream integrity and confidentiality shall be protected to prevent spoofing and privacy breach
Driver state classification shall be accurate and tamper-proof to ensure correct transition demand
Transition demand signal shall be authenticated to prevent suppression or false injection
MRM trigger shall be authenticated and protected from false injection or suppression
DMS firmware and AI model shall be verified before OTA update application
Diagnostic access to driver video data shall be restricted to authorized sessions

damage_scenarios.description: Drowsy driver not detected due to camera spoofing — ADAS continues without transition demand — collision occurs
Driver state message tampered — ADAS not notified of drowsy driver — no takeover requested
False MRM injection causes sudden vehicle stop in high-speed traffic
Driver biometric video data extracted — major privacy violation
DMS disabled via DoS or diagnostic exploit — driver monitoring completely non-functional

r157_specific_requirements: DMS shall detect driver unavailability within 4 second transition demand window
DMS shall issue optical, acoustic, and haptic warnings in escalation sequence
DMS shall trigger MRM if driver is unresponsive after full warning sequence
DMS shall model driver reaction time of 0.75 seconds for safety calculations
sae_j3061_lifecycle_phases: Concept phase — DMS cybersecurity goals defined per R157
Development — privacy by design for driver video data
Validation — camera spoofing tests, replay attack tests
Incident response — OTA patch via ara::ucm
relevant_clauses: clause-9 — Item definition and cybersecurity goals
